title: VBA button binding, macro availability, and runtime smoke
originSignal: Workbook buttons or shapes appear correct but the assigned macro is missing, renamed, hidden in another module, or fails at runtime.
riskPattern: Static workbook layout can look correct while OnAction points to a missing macro or the VBA project cannot be accessed by automation.
regressionChecks: Inventory workbook buttons, shapes, and OnAction targets.
Export VBA modules and lint public entry points.
Run macro import/export smoke only in Windows desktop Excel with trusted VBA project access.
Keep copied workbooks separate from source files before macro edits.
expectedEvidence: Button binding report resolves existing macros and flags missing OnAction targets.
VBA lint/export evidence lists modules, public procedures, and syntax-level concerns.
Runtime macro smoke records Windows Excel version and success/failure output.
boundaries: OpenXML button binding checks do not compile or execute VBA.
Runtime validation requires Windows Excel and Trust Center access to the VBA project object model.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Add a sanitized xlsm with one valid button macro, one missing OnAction target, and one harmless macro smoke procedure.
